title: List of significant changes made in the English Dub
display_title: List of significant changes made in the English Dub
wikitext:
[]: List of significant changes made in the English Dub?             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 28, 2008 10:39 am I have yet to see an actual listing of ALL significant changes that the English dub made. The problem is that its taken as "common knowledge" on assumption, that "of course you know all the mistakes the English dub made"....while I do think there are points that the English dub simply didn't "get it", I do sympathize that Evangelion is one of the most nuanced shows ever made and was being held up to a very high standard. Personally, from an *acting* point of view, I was happy that pretty much every cast member felt like actual "acting" and not what I call the "reading a translation off an index card" effect like in Ghost in the Shell 1. I mean, even Hyuga, Maya, and Aoba have distinct voices. But anyway, I figured it would be interesting to try and list out all of the changes (anything) between the English and Japanese dubs. Obviously, points where they had to change idioms and puns that have no direct translation are notable, but not actual "mistakes". (for our purposes, "how disgusting..." is considered a fairly accurate translation of the purposefully vague line "Kimochi warui"...) However, include not just translation errors, but when they may have delivered a line light-heartedly that was meant to be serious. *During the Asuka mind-rape scene in the Director's Cut of episode 23, there's a point where 5 times in a row, Asuka is tormented by flashbacks of herself acting arrogant; what the original dub does is have each of the other 5 voice actresses from the other cast members say the line "I"m Asuka, charmed huh?" in a sarcastic imitation of Asuka. The result is Misato's voice, Ritsuko's voice, Rei's voice, Maya's voice, etc. imitating Asuka in a taunting way. Either because of VA constraints or because they simply didn't notice, the English dub just loops Tiffany Grant's voice five times. *Infamously, the translation of the scene in End of Eva where Misato explains what exactly the relationship between Adam, Lilith, Angels, Evas, and humans is, was completely mangled and simply got it wrong. *In episode 18, Asuka walks angrily into the classroom, and offscreen, we can hear her angrily unpacking her bag. The English dub either got confused or felt like adding in humor, so they changed it to that the unpacking noises are actually Asuka hitting Shinji, with Shinji saying "ow my eye!" etc. *Because Tiffany Grant speaks German, she inserted a lot of random German dialogue for Asuka, what she calls the "Ricky Ricardo effect" of that whenever she's flustered she reverts to German. This is compared to the original Japanese dub, when she speaks German exactly twice in the entire series (and when she does its "Gelman" (German Engrish) ) *Platinum DVD's inserted Shinji exclaiming "bullshit!" in episode 5 (were there other dialogue changes in episodes 1-20 of Platinum?) *English and Japanese versions of End of Eva shift Gendo's final line to Ritsuko between "Ritsuko, I truly..." and "Ritsuko, the truth is...." (I don't consider this an actual "mistake" but for such an important line even the tiniest nuance kind of alters the meaning a little) anything else?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Nov 28, 2008 11:04 am It may be different, but I hardly consider the added German 'a mistake'. That's one change that I feel the dub actually improved upon the original. What was a mistake, however, was the added ridiculous 'splat' noise and the "Hit 'em again!" silliness, both in EOE.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Nov 28, 2008 12:07 pm In ep. 21, when Misato is released from custody, the dub (and the original subs) translated her "Kare wa? as "Where is he?" (asking about Kaji). This helped perpetuate the myth the she is the one who killed him. The Platinum subs corrected the line to "What about him?. Note: "Kare wa?" can mean "Where is he?" in the right context; in this case, however, they chose the "where" translation improperly. In episode 23, when Misato comes into Shinji's room, the dub changes her line, "This is all I can do for you." to "I want you to know that I'm here for you.", completely hiding her seductive intent. (The original and Platinum subs both got the line right.) Even those who are still in denial on this point should admit that this change was not helpful in interpreting her intent. In episode 26, they change Misato's line "The real world isn't so bad", to "this world isn't so bad", adding to the confusion over what choice Shinji was making. This error also existed in the original subs. The Platinum subs correct the line to "This world of reality isn't so bad". Hmmm....when you factor in the infamous Adam/Lilith confusion from EOE, a disproportionate number of the blown lines seem to be Misato's. V wrote: *Platinum DVD's inserted Shinji exclaiming "bullshit!" in episode 5 (were there other dialogue changes in episodes 1-20 of Platinum?)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 28, 2008 1:43 pm there's a lot of Misato mistakes simply because Misato has a proportionately large amount of dialogue okay so the running list is: Asuka speaks a lot more German in the English dub (I agree, and this is one of the reasons I like the English dub a lot, is they convey better how "German" Asuka is) Asuka mind rape scene simply loops Tiffany Grant's voice 5 times (this may have been because they couldn't get the other voice actresses back, but was probably simply a mistake) Infamously incorrect translation of the vital line in EoE where Misato explains Adam, Lilith, Angels, and humans humor injected into episode 18 scene where Asuka is offscreen unpacking her bag, so it sounds like she's hitting Shinji Shinji exclaims "Bullshit!" in episode 5 (of Platinum DVDs) Gendo's final line to Ritsuko shifts between "the truth is" and "I truly..." which is admittedly a minor change, and personally I feel they are equivalent, but for such a cryptic line it subtly changes the implications Add in a "splat" sound effect into End of Eva that wasn't originally there, some say crossing the line between "dubbing" and changing what Anno wanted; also add in the line "hit'em again" with flamethrower Misato's line in episode 23 "kare wa?" can ambiguously be translated as either "Where is he?" or "what about him?", and using "where is he?" gives a bit more of an implication she shot Kaji (Anno effectively stated she didn't) Episode 23 gives "I want you to know that I'm here for you", although the (possibly suggestive) "this is all I can do for you" would be more accurate episode 26 replaces the original "the real world isn't so bad" to "this world isn't so bad", adding to the confusion. Kaworu's line in Japanese (i forget the word?) can alternately be translated as "like" or "love" depending on context and is ambiguous, but ADV chose to go with "I love you" When Misato says in episode 2 "feel free to take advantage of anything here" when entering her appartment, ADV's English dub added the extra line "...except of course me, that is!" either because they didn't get it or were just trying to match mouth flaps, not realizing that it was supposed to be a suggestive line Yes I am aware that the Directors' Cut had entirely new audio tracks and the dialogue is actually shifted for everyone, particularly standing out is that they changed Ritsuko's "if she fails she won't pilot again" to an angry "if that bitch fails she won't pilot again!!!" but this was all a matter of tone and not explicit changes (yes I do want to list times they change the "mood" of a line drastically from the original, but I don't think that was *too* drastic to be considered an "error") but the thing that intrigues me is just that we paid so much attention to the Director's Cut episodes, and many here seem to ignore the English dub, that I wonder if Platinum 1-20 was ignored and there might be other changes we just didn't notice. Are we sure episode 5 was the only one that got changed? As for the End of Eva stuff, I have no problem with the "hit'em again!" line (it kind of fits the scene) and the "Splat!" was a little awkward. Also, as for the two times they drop F-bombs in End of Eva, I think that was more a matter of translation, i.e. it actually fit pretty well the emotional point of the scene and how exacerbated everyone is. (wait, one of these was Misato's "so-fucking-what-if-I'm-not-you?!"...what was the other one?) Further, there's one thing that I kept hearing about where in the original, Kaji gets shot but it segways into (off screen) Asuka slaps Shinji for saying Kaji is dead. The result is that instead of hearing a gunshot, we cut to the sound of Asuka slapping Shinji, with the handclap/slapping noise standing in for the gunshot. The ADV people UTTERLY misunderstood this, and in the audio commentary, actually say "we thought that sounded too much like a handclap, so we fixed it by making it a gunshot" However, I always heard these arguments out of context; in the *actual* episode 21, Kaji is shot (offscreen) and in the original, its a gunshot noise. It's only in Death&Rebirth that, because its arranged as a clip-show cutting between various scenes, that the Kaji death scene is followed by Asuka slapping Shinji because Kaji is dead; that actually happens in a later episode. And while this was clearly an embarrassing mistake (given the audio commentary) on ADV's part....frankly......who gives a frak about Death&Rebirth? Do you honestly sit down and watch Death&Rebirth as much as End of Eva? when I introduce new people to the series I flat out tell them to SKIP Death&Rebirth, and then just tell them that "by the way, Adam is fused to Gendo's hand, they show this in a one-shot from D&R") So while honestly a mistake, because its in D&R, I frankly just ignore it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 28, 2008 2:53 pm NemZ wrote: It may be different, but I hardly consider the added German 'a mistake'. That's one change that I feel the dub actually improved upon the original.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 28, 2008 2:56 pm Besides the Eva-02 startup in episode 8, and the stemutter phonecall in episode 22, when else?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 28, 2008 3:17 pm - "Hallo, Shinji. Guten Morgen!" (Episode 09) - "Erst!" (Episode 25')

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 28, 2008 3:48 pm oh that's just triffling....that's not "real dialogue", but yeah.... anyway can you think of any other *specific* things in which ADV significantly changed something?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 28, 2008 4:03 pm V wrote: oh that's just triffling....that's not "real dialogue", but yeah....

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 28, 2008 5:07 pm tacit acknowledgement that no one ever figured this out? because when I was first watching this off Adult Swim and considered "those shiny happy smart people on the actual fan-run websites", to be literally, literally akin to Fandom-Gods in their knowledge of the series, I always took it as received Holy Writ that "the English dub has filled with flaws", until I realized that I could only specifically cite a few. Maybe ADV & Co ignore fan input.....because rather than rational discussion, the fan boards are filled with lynch mobs ready to destroy them? As the old saying goes, you can get further with a kind word and a two-by-four, than with a two-by-four alone. well we wouldn't want to act like "V knows everything", but rather stick to the assumption that "you should google it"? Googling Evangelion leads back in one big circle to here (and "a circle has no end", Arkady) check them yourself instead of relying on others..

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Fri Nov 28, 2008 7:39 pm V wrote: ....(musing) maybe I will Rachel....maybe I will...watch and create a full listing of all changes....it would be ...useful....

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 28, 2008 8:10 pm V wrote: tacit acknowledgement that no one ever figured this out?

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Dec 01, 2008 11:12 pm V wrote: Also, as for the two times they drop F-bombs in End of Eva, I think that was more a matter of translation, i.e. it actually fit pretty well the emotional point of the scene and how exacerbated everyone is. (wait, one of these was Misato's "so-fucking-what-if-I'm-not-you?!"...what was the other one?)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 01, 2008 11:38 pm The Shinji one worked. The Misato one was really forced.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Sun Apr 29, 2012 5:27 pm I didn't notice a difference between the dub and sub on Misato's confusing EoE explanation. Were they both mistranslated? Maybe I have a version of the sub that is, and there's another, better one.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Apr 29, 2012 6:44 pm You do. By the way, I know for a fact that Gendou's silent line begins with “The truth is...” in the dub. I thought that was what he said in Japanese as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 30, 2012 12:04 pm Angel of Will wrote: I didn't notice a difference between the dub and sub on Misato's confusing EoE explanation. Were they both mistranslated? Maybe I have a version of the sub that is, and there's another, better one.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Mon Apr 30, 2012 9:41 pm The dub apparently added a whole "BLEEEHHH" line after Asuka kisses Shinji, which kind of undermines the point of the scene (she was so disgusted that he didn't try to make any sort of move - though, to be fair, he really couldn't have - that she was embarrassed).

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Mon Apr 30, 2012 9:48 pm I have to say that even on my first viewing of the series (which was in English), I understood the scene that way. I figured that Asuka only found disgust in the kiss after not having gotten anything out of it. Also, the Japanese dub, instead of the BLEAGH, has an 'ik! or a yuk!' or something similar immediately before her line, so I don't think it did much damage.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 30, 2012 9:52 pm Angel of Will wrote: I have to say that even on my first viewing of the series (which was in English), I understood the scene that way. I figured that Asuka only found disgust in the kiss after not having gotten anything out of it. Also, the Japanese dub, instead of the BLEAGH, has an 'ik! or a yuk!' or something similar immediately before her line, so I don't think it did much damage.

The problem is that its taken as "common knowledge" on assumption, that "of course you know all the mistakes the English dub made"....while I do think there are points that the English dub simply didn't "get it&q
